Ghostty Themes — WCAG 2.2 Accessibility Report

Theme Picker
Metrics:

WCAG 2.2 Accessibility Scoring

This report analyzes terminal color themes against the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 standard, using three success criteria related to color contrast.

WCAG 2.2 Success Criteria

SC 1.4.3 — Contrast Minimum (Level AA) Normal text: ≥ 4.5 : 1
Large text: ≥ 3.0 : 1
SC 1.4.6 — Contrast Enhanced (Level AAA) Normal text: ≥ 7.0 : 1
Large text: ≥ 4.5 : 1
SC 1.4.11 — Non-text Contrast (Level AA) UI components: ≥ 3.0 : 1
Applied to cursor & selection regions
Conformance Levels AAA = highest (enhanced)
AA = minimum recommended
AA-Large = large text only

Contrast Ratio Formula

Per WCAG 2.2 and the G17 technique:

1. Normalize sRGB (8-bit to 0–1):
  RsRGB = R8bit / 255

2. Linearize each channel:
  if RsRGB ≤ 0.04045 → R = RsRGB / 12.92
  else → R = ((RsRGB + 0.055) / 1.055)2.4

3. Relative luminance:
  L = 0.2126 × R + 0.7152 × G + 0.0722 × B

4. Contrast ratio (L1 = lighter, L2 = darker):
  CR = (L1 + 0.05) / (L2 + 0.05)
  Range: 1 : 1 (identical) to 21 : 1 (black on white)

What Colors Are Checked

Every theme defines up to 22 colors. We check 6 contrast pairs:

PairWCAG CriterionWhat it Tests
foreground vs backgroundSC 1.4.3 / 1.4.6Main text readability
16 palette colors vs backgroundSC 1.4.3 / 1.4.6ANSI color readability
cursor-color vs backgroundSC 1.4.11Cursor visibility
cursor-text vs cursor-colorSC 1.4.3 / 1.4.6Text under cursor
selection-foreground vs selection-backgroundSC 1.4.3 / 1.4.6Selected text readability
selection-background vs backgroundSC 1.4.11Selection distinguishability

Score Calculation (0–100)

Each component maps its contrast ratio to points. Ratio-based scores use min(ratio / 21, 1) × weight. Palette scores use the fraction of 16 colors passing the threshold.

ComponentFormulaMax
FG / BG contrastmin(ratio / 21, 1) × 3030 pts
Palette AA passes (≥ 4.5:1)(count / 16) × 2525 pts
Palette AAA passes (≥ 7:1)(count / 16) × 1515 pts
Cursor vs BGmin(ratio / 21, 1) × 1010 pts
Cursor text vs cursormin(ratio / 21, 1) × 55 pts
Selection textmin(ratio / 21, 1) × 1010 pts
Selection visibilitymin(ratio / 21, 1) × 55 pts
100 pts

When a color is absent (e.g., no cursor-color defined), half the possible points are awarded as a neutral default.

APCA — WCAG 3.0 Contrast (toggleable)

The APCA (Accessible Perceptual Contrast Algorithm) is the proposed replacement for WCAG 2.x contrast ratio in WCAG 3.0. Key differences:

  • Polarity-aware: light-on-dark and dark-on-light are scored differently (human vision is asymmetric)
  • Better perceptual model: uses tuned exponents instead of a simple luminance ratio
  • Font-size-aware: thresholds vary by text size and weight
Luminance: Y = 0.2126729·(R/255)2.4 + 0.7151522·(G/255)2.4 + 0.0721750·(B/255)2.4
Soft clamp: if Y < 0.022 → Y += (0.022 − Y)1.414
Normal (dark on light): Lc = ((bgY0.56 − txtY0.57) × 1.14 − 0.027) × 100
Reverse (light on dark): Lc = ((bgY0.65 − txtY0.62) × 1.14 + 0.027) × 100

Thresholds (at W400 normal weight): |Lc| ≥ 75 for body text (~18px), |Lc| ≥ 60 for large text (~24px), |Lc| ≥ 45 for headlines only.

Extra Metrics (toggleable)

MetricWhat it MeasuresFormula
Blue Light IndexBlue emission from background (0–1). Lower = warmer, easier on eyes at night.Blin / (Rlin + Glin + Blin)
CCT (K)Correlated Color Temperature. < 4000 K = warm, > 6500 K = cool/harsh.McCamy’s approximation from CIE xy chromaticity
Min DeltaEClosest pair of palette colors. Low = two colors look the same. > 10 is good.CIE76: √(ΔL² + Δa² + Δb²) in CIELAB
Hue CoverageHow spread out the palette hues are. Higher = richer variety. > 60° is good.Circular std. dev. of atan2(b*, a*) in CIELAB

These metrics are hidden by default. Toggle each to see values per card. Click Include in Score to blend them into the ranking:

ComponentEnhanced FormulaMax
WCAG scoreoriginal × 0.770 pts
Blue Light(1 − index) × 7.57.5 pts
CCT warmth(10000 − K) / 8000 × 7.57.5 pts
Min DeltaEmin(deltaE / 30, 1) × 7.57.5 pts
Hue Coveragemin(stddev / 120, 1) × 7.57.5 pts
100 pts

Filters & Layouts

  • Dark / Light — Filter by background luminance (dark < 0.5).
  • Grid — Default card grid, good for browsing.
  • List — Single-column, code preview side by side with details.
  • Compact — Dense grid, hides code preview for scanning.
